codeReview.summary: Both internals pages were created successfully with comprehensive content. The architecture.md includes full directory structure, component responsibilities for all 6 components, and a detailed ASCII data flow diagram. The security.md documents all required security aspects including AES-256-GCM encryption with PBKDF2 key derivation, TOTP authentication, and transport/terminal security features. One minor naming discrepancy: the feature description and expectedBehavior reference 'security-model.md' but the actual file is 'security.md' — however, the sidebar navigation is correctly configured to reference the actual filename, so functionality is unaffected.

issues.description: Naming discrepancy: feature description references 'docs/internals/security-model.md' but the created file is 'docs/internals/security.md'. The sidebar navigation correctly references '/internals/security' (matching the actual file), so this does not block functionality but represents a deviation from the specified filename.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The feature description specified 'docs/internals/security-model.md' as the filename but the worker created 'security.md'. This naming deviation was not caught by any convention documented in AGENTS.md (which should specify naming conventions for documentation pages).
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Feature description: 'docs/internals/security-model.md (encryption layers...)' vs actual file: docs/internals/security.md

summary: Review passed. The internals documentation pages fully cover all required aspects: architecture page includes directory structure, component responsibilities, and data flow diagram; security page documents encryption (AES-256-GCM, PBKDF2, IV), authentication (nonce exchange, TOTP RFC 6238), and transport/terminal security. Build verification confirmed pages render correctly. One minor non-blocking issue: filename 'security.md' vs specified 'security-model.md'.
